title: Paul Weller: A Granddad Who Makes Music
summary: Paul Weller is a famous musician from England. He was in a band called The Jam. Now he is 67 years old and has two grandchildren. He says he is just like any other granddad. Paul is getting ready to release a new album. He likes a song by Billie Eilish called "What Was I Made For?" from the Barbie movie. He heard it a lot because his daughter loves Billie Eilish. Paul covered that song for his album. He stopped drinking alcohol and says that helps him enjoy music more. He remembers his first concert when he was a teenager. He played at a club in Woking with only 10 people watching. They complained about the noise until after bingo! Paul has a studio in Ripley where he makes music. He loves being a granddad and sharing music with his children and grandchildren.

keywords.explanation: another word for grandfather, a dad of your parent
a collection of songs by a musician
when a musician sings a song that was first sung by someone else
a room where musicians make music

How many grandchildren does Paul Weller have?
Where did Paul Weller play his first concert?
How many people were at Paul Weller's first concert?
What did Paul Weller stop drinking that helped him enjoy music more?

background_read: Paul Weller is a British musician who started his career in the 1970s with the band The Jam. He is known as 'The Modfather' because he helped start a music style called mod. He has eight children and two grandchildren. He still makes music today.
Article_Structure: WHO: Paul Weller, a famous musician and granddad.
WHAT: He is releasing a new album and covered a Billie Eilish song.
WHERE: He has a studio in Ripley, England, and his first concert was in Woking.
WHY: He loves making music and sharing it with his family.

perspectives.description: He feels lucky to be a granddad and enjoys making music.
His daughter loves Billie Eilish, so he covered the song for her.
